message.content:
This parent section delivers a comprehensive, region-by-region analysis of 2025 global mobile game advertising trends, supplemented by industry expert commentary on market evolution and technological disruption. Covering the reporting period of January to December 2025, the content is structured to support retrieval queries on regional market scale, creative format dominance, top-performing titles, monthly campaign fluctuations, and strategic industry shifts.

**Topics & Metrics:** The section quantifies year-over-year growth for active advertisers and deduplicated ad creatives across eight regions: North America (+56% advertisers/7.1W, +67% creatives/14.2M), Japan & South Korea (+51%/61K, +55%/13.4M), China HK/Macau/TW (+26%/111K, +42%/19.7M), Southeast Asia (+52%/68K, +69%/11.7M), Middle East (+58%/21.1W, +54%/29.9M), Europe (+22%/67K, +34%/12.8M), Oceania (+40%/11.8W, +76%/19.6M), and South America (monthly advertiser volumes scaling 0–40K). Video creatives dominate globally, accounting for 70.2%–77.7% of ad formats depending on the region. Monthly tracking includes advertiser counts, active creative volumes, and new-entry ratios.

**Claims & Trends:** Key claims highlight regional strategic divergences: Europe shows slow growth with a pivot toward puzzle/casual genres and niche IP integration; South America demonstrates a consistent upward trend in creative refresh rates (new creative adoption rising from 66.0% in January to 83.8% in December); Southeast Asia notes Middle East titles peaking in May/June with slow iteration cycles (<40% new creative ratio); and HK/Macau/TW exhibits the lowest global video share but high acceptance of Chinese, Japanese, and Korean local IPs. Android leads globally in advertiser and creative volume.

**Entities & Products:** Trending titles are cataloged per region, including *Solitaire Associations*, *Journey*, and *X-Clash* (North America); *奇蹟MU*, *Mahjong Wonders*, and *杖剑传说* (Japan/Korea); *Lands of Jail*, *Top Heroes*, and *HAIKYU!! FLY HIGH* (HK/Macau/TW); *Kingshot*, *Mafia City*, and *Logicus* (Southeast Asia); *Vita Mahjong*, *Zen Color*, and *Umamusume: Pretty Derby* (Middle East); *Block Crush!*, *Paint by Number*, and *Dream Raiders* (Europe); and *Bible Word Puzzle*, *Rise of Kingdoms*, and *ACECRAFT* (Oceania). The primary data source is the 广大大 (SocialPeta) Advertiser Analysis Module.

**Figures & Data Structure:** Each regional subsection features monthly time-series charts (Jan–Dec 2025) plotting advertiser volume vs. new advertiser ratio, and active creative volume vs. new creative ratio. Additional visuals include system/platform distribution charts, metric summary cards, and ranked hot-product lists.

**Definitions, Procedures & Industry Commentary:** The concluding "Media Voices" segment addresses 2025–2026 market dynamics, defining hybrid monetization models (IAA, IAP, IAAP) and their role in lowering user barriers, expanding reach, optimizing LTV, and hedging UA costs. It outlines AI-driven production and marketing procedures, including rapid multi-style 2D/3D asset generation, player behavior profiling, personalized ad material creation, churn prediction, and recall campaign triggering. Strategic concepts covered include "A+B" gameplay fusion (e.g., SLG+casual), "sub-gameplay UA" strategies, SLG genre industrialization, and mini-game platform expansion (TikTok/Douyin).

**Evidence Types:** All regional insights are grounded in platform-sourced quantitative analytics, YoY growth percentages, monthly time-series metrics, and product rankings. Strategic and procedural claims are supported by aggregated industry expert commentary, market observations, and documented AI/marketing workflow pipelines.

   - *0135 (Europe):* 2025 trends, advertisers +22% (67K), creatives +34% (12.8M), video 76.8%, monthly charts, titles (Block Crush!, Paint by Number, etc.), slow growth, focus on puzzle/casual & niche IP, source: 广大大.
   - *0136 (Oceania):* 2025 trends, advertisers +40% (11.8W), creatives +76% (19.6M), video 74.2%, monthly charts, titles (Bible Word Puzzle, Rise of Kingdoms, etc.), Android leads, peak May/Aug, source: 广大大.

   **Topics & Metrics:** The section quantifies year-over-year growth for active advertisers and deduplicated ad creatives across eight regions: North America (+56% advertisers/7.1W, +67% creatives/14.2M), Japan & South Korea (+51%/61K, +55%/13.4M), China HK/Macau/TW (+26%/111K, +42%/19.7M), Southeast Asia (+52%/68K, +69%/11.7M), Middle East (+58%/21.1W, +54%/29.9M), Europe (+22%/67K, +34%/12.8M), Oceania (+40%/11.8W, +76%/19.6M), and South America (monthly advertiser volumes scaling 0–40K). Video creatives dominate globally, accounting for 70.2%–77.7% of ad formats depending on the region. Monthly tracking includes advertiser counts, active creative volumes, and new-entry ratios.

   **Claims & Trends:** Key claims highlight regional strategic divergences: Europe shows slow growth with a pivot toward puzzle/casual genres and niche IP integration; South America demonstrates a consistent upward trend in creative refresh rates (new creative adoption rising from 66.0% in January to 83.8% in December); Southeast Asia notes Middle East titles peaking in May/June with slow iteration cycles (<40% new creative ratio); and HK/Macau/TW exhibits the lowest global video share but high acceptance of Chinese, Japanese, and Korean local IPs. Android leads globally in advertiser and creative volume.

   **Entities & Products:** Trending titles are cataloged per region, including *Solitaire Associations*, *Journey*, and *X-Clash* (North America); *奇蹟MU*, *Mahjong Wonders*, and *杖剑传说* (Japan/Korea); *Lands of Jail*, *Top Heroes*, and *HAIKYU!! FLY HIGH* (HK/Macau/TW); *Kingshot*, *Mafia City*, and *Logicus* (Southeast Asia); *Vita Mahjong*, *Zen Color*, and *Umamusume: Pretty Derby* (Middle East); *Block Crush!*, *Paint by Number*, and *Dream Raiders* (Europe); and *Bible Word Puzzle*, *Rise of Kingdoms*, and *ACECRAFT* (Oceania). The primary data source is the 广大大 (SocialPeta) Advertiser Analysis Module.

   **Figures & Data Structure:** Each regional subsection features monthly time-series charts (Jan–Dec 2025) plotting advertiser volume vs. new advertiser ratio, and active creative volume vs. new creative ratio. Additional visuals include system/platform distribution charts, metric summary cards, and ranked hot-product lists.

   **Definitions, Procedures & Industry Commentary:** The concluding "Media Voices" segment addresses 2025–2026 market dynamics, defining hybrid monetization models (IAA, IAP, IAAP) and their role in lowering user barriers, expanding reach, optimizing LTV, and hedging UA costs. It outlines AI-driven production and marketing procedures, including rapid multi-style 2D/3D asset generation, player behavior profiling, personalized ad material creation, churn prediction, and recall campaign triggering. Strategic concepts covered include "A+B" gameplay fusion (e.g., SLG+casual), "sub-gameplay UA" strategies, SLG genre industrialization, and mini-game platform expansion (TikTok/Douyin).

   **Evidence Types:** All regional insights are grounded in platform-sourced quantitative analytics, YoY growth percentages, monthly time-series metrics, and product rankings. Strategic and procedural claims are supported by aggregated industry expert commentary, market observations, and documented AI/marketing workflow pipelines.
